steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content:
The sentence reads: "Popper's theory ______ that scientific hypotheses must be refutable, termed the criterion of falsifiability, rejects the confirmationist position..."

The main verb of this sentence is rejects. The blank must fill the slot between the subject (Popper's theory) and that main verb without creating a second, competing main verb.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — was dictating: past-progressive main verb. Creates two main verbs (was dictating and rejects) with no conjunction to join them — a run-on. ✗
  • Choice B — dictated: simple past main verb. Same double-main-verb problem; a participial reading of dictated would require a passive construction that doesn't fit here. ✗
  • Choice C — dictating: present participle. Produces "Popper's theory, dictating that...falsifiability, rejects..." — one main verb (rejects), with dictating as a participial phrase modifying the subject. ✓
  • Choice D — dictates: simple-present main verb. Same double-main-verb problem as A and B. ✗
That is the correct answer.
